w:r.w:t: LinkedIn Automation Agent
Developer Test Task
EVALUATION TASK
Task 1: Total Estimate
Estimate total time (hours) for building LinkedIn Agent (1 FTE dev).
Task 2: Feature Breakdown
List all features/scope items with effort estimate for each.
Task 3: Clear vs. Uncertain
List what you know how to build immediately. List what is uncertain or requires research.
Task 4: Product Questions
List all questions/concerns/proposals about the product.
Task 5: Tech Stack
What stack would you use to build this?
PRODUCT OVERVIEW

Automation platform that manages 500-1000 LinkedIn accounts from Adspower. System spreads connection requests across accounts (15-20 per day per account), keeps accounts active through farming (likes, comments, follows), and centralizes all replies in one unified inbox. Every action—connections, likes, comments, follows, response times—is randomized and unique per account to mimic genuine human behavior. No templating, no mechanical patterns, no duplicated actions. Accounts stay healthy via activity monitoring and realistic behavior variations (random timing, occasional skips, natural delays). User manages everything from one dashboard: deploy campaigns, monitor inbox, track account health.
CORE FEATURE 1: AUTO-CONNECTION & SPREAD
What it does:
System automatically spreads personalized connection requests across 300-1000 accounts over extended periods. Each account sends at human-like pace to avoid LinkedIn ban triggers.
How it works:
Admin
uploads a contact list (CSV: Name, Company, Title, LinkedIn URL)
System distributes contacts across the 300-1000 accounts
Each account sends 15-20 connection requests per day with randomized timing (3-7 minute gaps)
Across 500 accounts: ~7,500-10,000 daily requests spread naturally over weeks/months
Each request includes a personalized message: "Hi [Name], I came across your profile and noticed you work in [Industry]..."
Messages vary: different openings, different pain points, different perspectives—no templated patterns
Send timing is human-like: varies by hour, sometimes skips a day, occasional evening sends
System tracks: pending -> sent -> accepted -> replied -> dead
User sees live dashboard: "500 pending | 45 accepted today | 8 replied"
Ban prevention:
Capping at 15-20 requests per account per day (never exceeds)
Randomizing send times (never exact same hour daily)
Adding natural delays (3-7 seconds between actions)
Monitoring for LinkedIn signals: slow responses, CAPTCHAs, rate limits
Auto-pausing an account immediately if detected (6-12 hour cooldown)
Building in sleep days: each account skips 1-2 days per week (human behavior)
Monitoring Social Selling Index (SSI): if drops below 70, activity reduces automatically
CORE FEATURE 2: ACCOUNT FARMING (CREDIBILITY BUILDING)
What it does:

While connection requests spread over time, accounts must stay active and credible. System auto-likes, auto-comments, and auto-follows—but every action is unique and human-like. An active account gets 40-60% connection acceptance; inactive accounts get 5-10%.
How it works:
Auto-Liking

Each day, 5-10 posts are auto-liked (randomly selected, never the same ones). Likes happen throughout the day—one every 90 minutes with variation. Every account likes different posts based on their unique feed. This mimics genuine industry engagement.
Auto-Commenting

When the system likes a post, it reads the content and generates a thoughtful, contextual comment. Not spam ("Great post!"), but real: "This resonates—especially the point about reducing CAC. We have seen 30% improvement by focusing on that metric." AI understands the post topic and writes naturally. User sets tone (formal, casual, technical) and system adapts. Comments post 30-60 minutes after the like (mimics real reading -> thinking -> commenting).
Auto-Following

System auto-follows 3-5 relevant people daily. Targets are user-defined: title="VP Sales", company="Fortune 500", location="USA", or custom filters. Every account follows different people based on their own network and interests. Follows happen throughout the day, building authentic networks.
CORE FEATURE 3: UNIFIED INBOX
What it does:
All 300-1000 accounts LinkedIn inboxes are merged into one central dashboard. User sees every message, reply, and engagement from all accounts without logging in/out.
How it works:
Single Inbox View
System pulls all messages from all 300-1000 accounts every 5 minutes
Displays as searchable, filterable table
Each row: Sender Name | Company | Message preview | Time received | Sentiment | Account source
Filtering & Search
Filter by new/unread messages
Replying
User clicks "Reply" on any message
Types response in compose box
System sends from the correct account automatically
No account switching needed
All sent messages logged in unified inbox
Real-Time Monitoring
Dashboard KPIs: "Messages sent today: 7,500 | Replies received: 340 | Active accounts: 295/300 | Hot leads: 12"
Drill down: "Which account got most replies?" "Which at ban risk?"
Visual heatmap: green (healthy), yellow (at risk), red (paused)
